Issue / Opportunity: Page contains two <h1> tags (lines 366 and 382). Search engines treat this as a structure error and split keyword authority.
Recommended Action: Demote the second <h1> to <h2>. Keep the primary H1 as 'Get Your Free Installation Estimate' or strengthen with location.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Single-H1 pages signal one main topic to Google. Fixing avoids a structural penalty.
Notes: Demoted second <h1> at line 382 to <h2>. estimate.html now has exactly 1 H1 (the page-hero title) and 12 H2s. Browser-default styling on the .hero block carries over via global h2 rule (Barlow Condensed clamp 28-48px). Visual hierarchy now correct: page H1 dominates, tool sub-heading is H2. Modified files: estimate.html

Issue / Opportunity: Page is 100% placeholder copy. Six review cards say '[REPLACE THIS WITH A REAL REVIEW]'. AggregateRating + Review schema is commented out waiting for real data.
Recommended Action: Collect 5–10 real Google / HomeStars reviews with permission. Replace placeholders. Uncomment AggregateRating + Review schema. Wire up to Google Business Profile if possible.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Social proof is the single biggest local-SEO + conversion lever. Rich-result review stars in SERP add 30–50% CTR. No real reviews = no review-snippet eligibility.
Notes: BLOCKED on owner input. Patrick must provide 5–10 real Google / HomeStars review texts (with reviewer permission to publish). Once received, Marketer formats and inserts into reviews.html, then Developer uncomments the AggregateRating + Review JSON-LD. Will move to Complete in Phase 1 retro once unblocked.

Issue / Opportunity: Form submission across 6 forms used mailto: link (3 active mailto handlers + 2 fake-submit blog forms that captured nothing + the no-handler quote builder also fell through to mailto). Opens user email client; data is lost if user closes browser. No backend persistence; no success/failure feedback.
Recommended Action: Replace mailto: with a proper form handler — Formspree / Basin / Netlify Forms / Resend.com. Add success and error states. Optionally store in a Google Sheet via webhook.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Email-based form is unreliable and unprofessional. Lead loss rate likely 40–60% on this form.
Notes: COMPLETE. All 6 active forms across the site now wired to Formspree endpoint mvzdjolv via fetch() AJAX: contact form (contact.html), Same-Day Quick-Book (index.html), Installation Estimator (estimate.html — both duplicate handlers replaced), Sprinkler Quote Builder (js/sprinkler-builder.js), and the 2 previously-fake-submit blog forms (newsletter signup + ask-the-pro). Each form posts a distinct _subject so the owner can sort leads by source in the Formspree dashboard. Each also includes a _gotcha honeypot for spam protection and a _source field for funnel attribution. All preserve their original UI patterns (estimate.html still calls goStep(5) on success; quote builder still calls showSuccess() on success; contact + quick-book have branded in-page success / error blocks; blog forms swap submit button into a confirmation chip). Old quote-legacy.html still has mailto handler but is robots-disallowed and orphaned per handoff. Modified files: contact.html, index.html, estimate.html, blog.html, js/sprinkler-builder.js, style.css (sd-bridge result styles).

Issue / Opportunity: 8.1 MB SVG file embedded on homepage. SVGs that large usually contain raster image data — defeats the purpose of vector format.
Recommended Action: Open in editor and either re-export as optimized SVG (under 200 KB) OR convert to JPG/WebP at 80% quality. Update any references.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Single 8 MB asset can dominate page-weight on slow connections. Reducing to <300 KB removes a Lighthouse performance flag.
Dependencies: Need design source (Illustrator / Figma) OR raster fallback
Notes: SVG was 100%% base64-wrapped JPEG (8005 KB total, 8004 KB raster). Extracted embedded image (3024x4032 source), downscaled to 1100x1466 (retina-friendly for the 527x702 display element), re-encoded as progressive JPEG quality 85. Final: 508 KB (94% reduction). Updated index.html line 1439 reference from .svg to .jpg + added loading=lazy. Old SVG deleted from worktree and project root. Modified files: index.html, about-section-picture.svg (deleted), about-section-picture.jpg (created).

Issue / Opportunity: Only 9 of ~105 images use loading='lazy'. Below-fold images load in the critical path unnecessarily.
Recommended Action: Add loading='lazy' to every below the first viewport. Keep eager loading on hero images only.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Cuts mobile data usage. Improves Largest Contentful Paint (LCP).

Issue / Opportunity: Hero images are served at full resolution to all devices regardless of viewport. No <picture> or srcset.
Recommended Action: Generate 3 sizes per hero (mobile 768w / tablet 1280w / desktop 2000w) and use srcset with sizes attribute. Or use CSS image-set() for backgrounds.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Mobile users currently download desktop-size images. Lighthouse flag.

Issue / Opportunity: 11 distinct media-query breakpoints across the site (1024 / 1000 / 900 / 880 / 768 / 760 / 720 / 640 / 600 / 540 / 520 / 480). Some redundant.
Recommended Action: Consolidate to 4 core breakpoints: 1024 (desktop) / 768 (tablet) / 640 (large mobile) / 480 (mobile). Audit and merge overlapping rules.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Easier to maintain / fewer surprises when adjusting layouts. Reduces mistakes when one breakpoint is updated and similar ones aren't.
Notes: Major refactor — should be done with full visual regression check across all pages and breakpoints.

Issue / Opportunity: Navigation block (~110 lines) and footer (~100 lines) are duplicated across all 28 HTML files. Any change requires 28 edits — high error rate / drift risk.
Recommended Action: Migrate to a static-site generator (Astro / 11ty / Hugo) with shared partials. Alternative low-effort fix: server-side include via a build-time concat script.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Single biggest maintainability win. Future-proofs the site for whoever maintains it after the current owner.
Notes: Astro is recommended for this stack: keeps the static-output / no-server simplicity but adds component reuse.

Page / Area: FAQPage schema for new service pages
File Name: Spring Opening / Fall Winterization / Repair pages (when built)
Issue / Opportunity: FAQPage schema is excellent on existing area pages and faq.html. New service pages should match.
Recommended Action: Add FAQPage JSON-LD with 3–5 service-specific questions to each new service page (cost / timing / DIY-risk / warranty / process).
Reason / Expected Benefit: FAQ rich-results in SERP. High-CTR snippet style.

Issue / Opportunity: Visible breadcrumb UI exists but no BreadcrumbList JSON-LD. Misses the rich-result eligibility.
Recommended Action: Add BreadcrumbList to each area page: Home → Service Areas → [City].
Reason / Expected Benefit: Helps Google understand hierarchy. Breadcrumb in SERP.

Issue / Opportunity: Once 5+ verified reviews are live (see ID 2), homepage should expose AggregateRating. Currently absent.
Recommended Action: Add AggregateRating JSON-LD with ratingValue / reviewCount once reviews are live. Update reviewCount monthly.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Star rating in local-pack and SERP. Major CTR lift.
Notes: Don't fake / inflate. Manual penalty risk.

File Name: nav.js + nav HTML in all pages
Issue / Opportunity: Hamburger button has no aria-expanded attribute. Screen readers can't announce open/closed state.
Recommended Action: Add aria-expanded='false' (toggled to 'true' on open) to .nav-hamburger. Add aria-controls pointing to .nav-mobile id.
Reason / Expected Benefit: WCAG compliance. Screen reader users currently can't tell if menu is open.

Issue / Opportunity: Footer social-icon links contain only a single character (e.g., 'f' for Facebook). Screen readers read 'f link'.
Recommended Action: Add aria-label='Facebook' / aria-label='Instagram' / etc. to each social link. Or wrap visible text in <span class='visually-hidden'>.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Screen reader accessibility. Common WCAG fix.

Issue / Opportunity: Decorative inline SVG icons (logo-mark / etc.) lack aria-hidden='true'. Screen readers may announce them.
Recommended Action: Add aria-hidden='true' to all decorative SVG sprites used as icons.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Cleaner screen-reader experience. WCAG-A polish.

Issue / Opportunity: Page uses generic phrasing ('takes 30 seconds') that drifts from the brand's honest / non-pushy voice.
Recommended Action: Rewrite intro and CTA copy in PJL's existing tone. Look at FAQ and About pages for tone reference.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Brand consistency = trust. Mismatched copy reads as templated / lazy.
Notes: Can be done at the same time as ID 2.

Issue / Opportunity: Page uses emoji icons heavily (🏡 🔒 🌿 ✨). Friendlier than the rest of the site but inconsistent with the professional / clean tone.
Recommended Action: Replace emoji with the brand's existing inline-SVG icon system used on other pages (water drop / sparkle pattern in nav.html).
Reason / Expected Benefit: Visual consistency across pages. Inline SVGs render at any size and respect color tokens.

Issue / Opportunity: Homepage hero has CTA + value prop but no immediate social proof above the fold.
Recommended Action: Add a single testimonial quote + name + rating beneath the hero — once real reviews are live (see ID 2).
Reason / Expected Benefit: First-impression trust. Most visitors don't scroll past the fold.

Issue / Opportunity: Only Patrick is shown. If there's a crew, photos build trust and signal that bookings won't be solo-trader-overrun.
Recommended Action: Add 1–2 photos of crew at work (with permission). Caption with first names.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Humanizes the company. Reassures buyers who worry the owner can't scale.

Issue / Opportunity: Caledon (~70k) — northwestern GTA. Lower density / larger lots = higher avg project value.
Recommended Action: Create page emphasizing estate-property positioning.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Premium-positioning play. Lower volume / higher AOV.

Issue / Opportunity: Subset of broader Stouffville coverage. May be redundant with ID 18.
Recommended Action: Decide if ID 18 covers this or build separately.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Geographic completeness.
Notes: Probably skip if Stouffville page is comprehensive.

Issue / Opportunity: Innisfil already mentioned in FAQ as service area. Needs a dedicated page to back the claim.
Recommended Action: Create page using template. Localize for Innisfil / Cookstown / Lefroy.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Schema-claim consistency. Captures Innisfil-specific searches.

Issue / Opportunity: CANCELLED — original recommendation was a backflow-testing service page. PJL does NOT hold an Ontario backflow tester certification. Building this page would have been false advertising.
Recommended Action: Cancelled. Replaced with: clear notice on Spring Opening + Fall Winterization fineprint stating Backflow Cert. Extra Charge (separate certified trade, refer-out only). Visible-content backflow claims removed from about.html (credential card + JSON-LD), index.html knowsAbout schema, blog-spring-sprinkler-opening.html (test claim corrected to visual check + referral), and blog-sprinkler-installation-newmarket.html (3 claims corrected to coordinate-with-certified-tester language).
Reason / Expected Benefit: Captures niche compliance traffic. High-trust signal (technical credential).
Notes: See memory/backflow_not_certified.md for the rule. Future Claude sessions must NOT recreate this recommendation. The correct service offering is a referral relationship with a certified Ontario backflow tester, not in-house testing.

Issue / Opportunity: Schema mentions commercial properties but no commercial landing page. Likely 20%+ of revenue but invisible to commercial property managers searching.
Recommended Action: Create page with commercial use cases (HOA / strip mall / multi-tenant residential / municipal). Mention of WSIB / liability insurance is a commercial trust signal.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Captures commercial / property-management traffic. Higher AOV.

Issue / Opportunity: 'How deep should sprinkler lines be buried' is a consistent search. No matching content.
Recommended Action: Write technical post on Ontario / York Region burial-depth requirements. Link to install + winterization services.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Compliance-related authority signal. Solid mid-volume search.

Issue / Opportunity: 'Best smart sprinkler controller' is high-intent shopping. Currently Hydrawise-only message — comparison post defends choice without defensiveness.
Recommended Action: Write Hunter Hydrawise vs Rain Bird ESP-Me feature / cost / support comparison.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Captures competitor-research traffic before they decide.

Issue / Opportunity: No professional handoff documentation currently exists. If the owner steps away the site is undocumented.
Recommended Action: Create comprehensive handoff doc (delivered as part of this audit). Maintain quarterly.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Continuity of operations. Future-proofing.

Issue / Opportunity: No phased SEO / content roadmap currently exists.
Recommended Action: Create phased plan (delivered as part of this audit).
Reason / Expected Benefit: Roadmap for owner / future marketer.

Issue / Opportunity: No central tracker for all open SEO / technical / content items.
Recommended Action: Maintain this CSV as items are completed. Update Status / Date Completed columns.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Single source of truth for progress.
Notes: This file. Update weekly.

Issue / Opportunity: Several inline style='...' attributes exist (footer link colors / stat font-size / skip-link absolute positioning). Should be class-based.
Recommended Action: Replace with utility classes or component CSS in style.css. Remove inline styles.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Cleaner separation of concerns. Easier theme adjustments.

Issue / Opportunity: Loads 14 weights of Barlow Condensed + DM Sans. Network cost ~80–120 KB.
Recommended Action: Audit which weights are actually used in CSS. Drop unused weights from the @import URL.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Faster initial paint. Smaller font payload.

Issue / Opportunity: Google Business Profile is the largest local-SEO factor. Status / completeness unknown to this audit (external system).
Recommended Action: Owner: ensure GBP is verified / has hours / has 10+ photos / weekly posts / categories accurately set / reviews pursued. Add UTM-tagged links to website.
Reason / Expected Benefit: GBP drives 60–80% of local-pack visibility. Free.

Issue / Opportunity: Local citations on Yelp / HomeStars / TrustedPros / BBB / Yellow Pages / York Region Chamber etc. matter for local pack.
Recommended Action: Audit current citation profile. Build out missing reputable directories. Ensure NAP (Name / Address / Phone) is consistent.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Citation consistency is a top-5 local-pack ranking factor.
Notes: Tools like BrightLocal / Whitespark help. Or do manually for top 10.

Issue / Opportunity: Link profile likely thin (small business / new site). Local-business backlinks accelerate ranking.
Recommended Action: Pursue: York Region Chamber listing / local newspaper supplier directory / Hunter dealer locator / supplier (Hydrawise) website / charity sponsorships.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Authority signal. Compounds over time.

Issue / Opportunity: Plain HTML site is fine but maintenance scales linearly with page count. Once 30+ pages exist nav / footer changes are painful.
Recommended Action: Migrate to Astro (recommended) — preserves static output / SEO friendly / shared components / Markdown blog.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Drastically improves maintainability. Speeds up future feature work.
Notes: Major project. Wait until next major redesign or after 40+ pages.

Issue / Opportunity: No visual map showing service radius. Geographic clarity helps prospects self-qualify.
Recommended Action: Embed Google Map with marked service area (or a stylized SVG map). Add to contact page or new dedicated page.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Reduces out-of-area inquiries. Visual trust.

Issue / Opportunity: Sitemap has '<lastmod>' values. These should update when pages are meaningfully changed.
Recommended Action: After any major page change update lastmod. Ideally automate as part of a build script.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Helps Google know when to re-crawl.

Issue / Opportunity: Comments at bottom of sitemap mention pending pages (winterization / commercial / gallery / additional area pages). When those pages are built they need to be added to sitemap.xml.
Recommended Action: On launch of each new page from this tracker — append <url> entry to sitemap. Resubmit sitemap in Google Search Console.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Ensures crawl coverage. Without sitemap entry pages may take weeks to be indexed.

Issue / Opportunity: Name / Address / Phone should be byte-identical across the site and external citations.
Recommended Action: Audit all 28 HTML pages and external listings (GBP / Yelp / etc.). Standardize on a single canonical NAP block.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Consistency is a ranking factor for local pack.

Issue / Opportunity: hreflang en-ca already set. If Patrick offers French-language service (Quebec / Ontarian francophones) consider FR variants.
Recommended Action: Skip unless French-language market is a real opportunity. Otherwise leave as is.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Not currently applicable.
Notes: Likely not applicable. Documented for completeness.

Issue / Opportunity: Hero eyebrow currently reads "SAME-DAY REPAIR · YEAR-ROUND SERVICE". The "Year-Round Service" claim is hard to justify for sprinkler repair specifically — Newmarket/GTA systems are winterized October to April, so 5-6 months of the year there is no active irrigation to repair (controller faults and indoor shut-off leaks are exceptions, ~5% of calls). Customers reading "year-round" reasonably expect a January repair tech, which is not what PJL actually offers. Risk: false-promise framing damages the honest brand voice.
Recommended Action: Replace the hero eyebrow text in sprinkler-repair.html. CONFIRMED REPLACEMENT (owner picked option 3, 2026-04-28): "SAME-DAY REPAIR · NEWMARKET & GTA". Search the file for class="repair-eyebrow" — the span currently contains "Same-Day Repair · Year-Round Service". Replace with "Same-Day Repair · Newmarket & GTA". No other markup changes needed.
Reason / Expected Benefit: Brand-tone consistency. Removes a claim PJL cannot reliably back up. The 1-year guarantee version doubles down on a real differentiator. Estimated impact: trust signal strength preserved without overstating service availability.
Notes: Logged 2026-04-28 from Patrick. Owner confirmed replacement copy: "Same-Day Repair · Newmarket & GTA" (option 3 of 3). Ready to apply on next sprinkler-repair.html edit. Change is queued, not blocking — will be picked up automatically next time the repair page is touched.
